title: Off the Ledge

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A sharp Iran de-escalation — Trump cancels the strikes, extends the ceasefire sixty days, oil eases and the Dow rallies near 930 points — pulls the day up off last week's lows into a mixed, faintly-relieved middle, even as a three-year-high 6.5% wholesale inflation print, a further night of Belfast riots, Ebola past 127 dead and famine spreading in North Darfur keep the relief short of good.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — vibraphone (VCSL), sampled piano (filtered as Rhodes-ish electric piano), RolandTR909 drum machine; supporting sine sub-bass and sawtooth pad
pattern: stack(s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ hh ~ hh ~ ~ hh ~ ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.4).hpf(55).room(0.18).rsize(4).swingBy(1/3, 4), note("g1 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ d2 ~ ~ c2 ~ ~ ~ ~ e1 ~").s("sine").attack(0.02).release(0.85).lpf(380).gain(0.45), note("<[g3,b3,d4,f4] [c3,e3,g3] [a2,c3,e3] [e3,g3,b3,d4]>").s("piano").lpf(sine.range(700,1700).slow(14)).gain(0.34).room(0.4).rsize(5).pan(0.45).delay(0.14).delaytime(0.33).slow(2), note("<[g4,d5,f5] [g4,c5,e5] [a4,c5,e5] [g4,b4,e5]>").s("sawtooth").attack(3).decay(1).sustain(0.4).release(4.5).lpf(sine.range(500,1200).slow(24)).gain(0.07).room(0.6).rsize(8), note("d5 ~ ~ b4 ~ ~ ~ d5 ~ ~ e5 ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ g4 ~ ~ a4 ~ b4 ~ ~ ~ ~ a4 ~ ~ ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.38).room(0.46).rsize(6).pan(0.55).delay(0.18).delaytime(0.41).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.12).pan(0.66).lpf(1800)).gain(sine.range(0.3,0.42).slow(16)))

lines: Word came down the strikes were called off, the strait swings open at last,
sixty days bought, the oil eased back, the board turned green and ran —
a nine-hundred-point exhale, a deal in 'pretty final shape',
and the breath you'd been holding all week finally found a place to land.
But the bill for the war came in the mail — six-and-a-half on the wholesale line,
and Belfast's still lit, the cannons up, the fever won't break tonight,
Darfur goes hungrier, the Ebola count climbs past a hundred and seven names —
so I hold the relief in a loosened hand, knowing it isn't all right.
We came off the ledge today, not up to the light, just off the worst of the fall,
an uneasy calm, a partial mercy, a ceasefire you don't quite trust,
I'll take the green on the screen and the deal half-signed, and the rest I'll carry too —
the riots, the hunger, the count still rising, and the small good news in the dust.
